verses.text: Some shall see the kingdom before death; on the mount He is transfigured, His raiment shining, with Elias and Moses. Glory unveiled. The hidden majesty of Christ shines forth.
Some shall see the kingdom before death; on the mount He is transfigured, His raiment shining, with Elias and Moses. Glory unveiled. The hidden majesty of Christ shines forth.
Some shall see the kingdom before death; on the mount He is transfigured, His raiment shining, with Elias and Moses. Glory unveiled. The hidden majesty of Christ shines forth.

Peter would build three tents; a cloud says, 'This is my beloved Son: hear him'; they see 'Jesus only.' Hear the Son. Above all others, Christ is to be heard.
Peter would build three tents; a cloud says, 'This is my beloved Son: hear him'; they see 'Jesus only.' Hear the Son. Above all others, Christ is to be heard.
Peter would build three tents; a cloud says, 'This is my beloved Son: hear him'; they see 'Jesus only.' Hear the Son. Above all others, Christ is to be heard.
Peter would build three tents; a cloud says, 'This is my beloved Son: hear him'; they see 'Jesus only.' Hear the Son. Above all others, Christ is to be heard.
Tell no man till He is risen; Elias has come already, in John. Forerunner fulfilled. The promised Elijah prepared the way and was rejected.
Tell no man till He is risen; Elias has come already, in John. Forerunner fulfilled. The promised Elijah prepared the way and was rejected.
Tell no man till He is risen; Elias has come already, in John. Forerunner fulfilled. The promised Elijah prepared the way and was rejected.

A father brings his son with a dumb spirit, whom the disciples could not cast out. Helpless need. He turns to Christ where others failed.
A father brings his son with a dumb spirit, whom the disciples could not cast out. Helpless need. He turns to Christ where others failed.
A father brings his son with a dumb spirit, whom the disciples could not cast out. Helpless need. He turns to Christ where others failed.

A father brings his son with a dumb spirit, whom the disciples could not cast out. Helpless need. He turns to Christ where others failed.
'O faithless generation'; the spirit oft casts him into fire; 'if thou canst do any thing... help us.' Faltering faith. The father's hope is mixed with doubt.
'O faithless generation'; the spirit oft casts him into fire; 'if thou canst do any thing... help us.' Faltering faith. The father's hope is mixed with doubt.
'O faithless generation'; the spirit oft casts him into fire; 'if thou canst do any thing... help us.' Faltering faith. The father's hope is mixed with doubt.
'O faithless generation'; the spirit oft casts him into fire; 'if thou canst do any thing... help us.' Faltering faith. The father's hope is mixed with doubt.
'If thou canst believe, all things are possible'; he cries, 'Lord, I believe; help thou mine unbelief.' Honest prayer. Even weak faith, owned, finds Christ's help.
'If thou canst believe, all things are possible'; he cries, 'Lord, I believe; help thou mine unbelief.' Honest prayer. Even weak faith, owned, finds Christ's help.
He rebukes the spirit; the child lies as dead, but Jesus lifts him, and he arises. Power and gentleness. He casts out the evil and raises the boy.
He rebukes the spirit; the child lies as dead, but Jesus lifts him, and he arises. Power and gentleness. He casts out the evil and raises the boy.
He rebukes the spirit; the child lies as dead, but Jesus lifts him, and he arises. Power and gentleness. He casts out the evil and raises the boy.
Why could they not cast it out? 'This kind... but by prayer and fasting.' Deeper need. Some victories require a faith fed by prayer.
Why could they not cast it out? 'This kind... but by prayer and fasting.' Deeper need. Some victories require a faith fed by prayer.
He foretells His death and rising; they understand not, and fear to ask. Slow to learn. They grasp the sorrow but not the hope.
He foretells His death and rising; they understand not, and fear to ask. Slow to learn. They grasp the sorrow but not the hope.
He foretells His death and rising; they understand not, and fear to ask. Slow to learn. They grasp the sorrow but not the hope.
Disputing who is greatest, He says, 'if any... be first... let him be... servant of all.' True greatness. The lowest place is the highest in the kingdom.
Disputing who is greatest, He says, 'if any... be first... let him be... servant of all.' True greatness. The lowest place is the highest in the kingdom.
Disputing who is greatest, He says, 'if any... be first... let him be... servant of all.' True greatness. The lowest place is the highest in the kingdom.
He sets a child among them; 'whosoever shall receive one of such children in my name receiveth me.' Welcomed least. To honour the lowly is to honour Christ.
He sets a child among them; 'whosoever shall receive one of such children in my name receiveth me.' Welcomed least. To honour the lowly is to honour Christ.
John forbade one casting out devils; 'forbid him not... he that is not against us is on our part.' No narrowness. Christ owns service done in His name beyond our circle.
John forbade one casting out devils; 'forbid him not... he that is not against us is on our part.' No narrowness. Christ owns service done in His name beyond our circle.
John forbade one casting out devils; 'forbid him not... he that is not against us is on our part.' No narrowness. Christ owns service done in His name beyond our circle.
A cup of water in His name shall not lose its reward; woe to him that offends a little one. Small kindness, great care. God notes the least service and the least believer.
A cup of water in His name shall not lose its reward; woe to him that offends a little one. Small kindness, great care. God notes the least service and the least believer.
If hand or foot offend, cut it off; better maimed in life than whole in hell. Decisive war. Sin must be cut away whatever the cost.
If hand or foot offend, cut it off; better maimed in life than whole in hell. Decisive war. Sin must be cut away whatever the cost.
If hand or foot offend, cut it off; better maimed in life than whole in hell. Decisive war. Sin must be cut away whatever the cost.
Better to enter the kingdom with one eye than be cast into hell, 'where their worm dieth not.' Solemn warning. The loss of the soul is the greatest loss of all.
Better to enter the kingdom with one eye than be cast into hell, 'where their worm dieth not.' Solemn warning. The loss of the soul is the greatest loss of all.
Better to enter the kingdom with one eye than be cast into hell, 'where their worm dieth not.' Solemn warning. The loss of the soul is the greatest loss of all.
Every one shall be salted with fire; 'have salt in yourselves, and have peace one with another.' Seasoned lives. Grace makes the Christian both pure and at peace.
